title: Build a Hermes Plugin
summary: Complete step-by-step guide for building a Hermes plugin with custom tools, lifecycle hooks, slash commands, CLI subcommands, bundled skills, and data files, covering plugin.yaml manifest, tool schemas, handler functions, and the registration API.

env_vars.name: HERMES_PLUGINS_DEBUG
env_vars.purpose: Enable verbose plugin discovery logging on stderr

cli_commands.purpose: List all discovered plugins and their status
Install a plugin and prompt for missing env vars
Debug plugin discovery with verbose output

slash_commands.purpose: Show loaded plugins and their tool/hook counts

code_examples.caption: plugin.yaml manifest
code_examples.code:
name: calculator
version: 1.0.0
description: Math calculator — evaluate expressions and convert units
provides_tools:
  - calculate
  - unit_convert
provides_hooks:
  - post_tool_call
requires_env:
  - name: WEATHER_API_KEY
    description: "API key for OpenWeather"
    url: "https://openweathermap.org/api"
    secret: true

code_examples.caption: Tool schema definition
Tool handler function
Plugin registration
code_examples.code:
def register(ctx):
    ctx.register_tool(name="calculate", toolset="calculator",
                      schema=schemas.CALCULATE, handler=tools.calculate)
    ctx.register_hook("post_tool_call", _on_post_tool_call)
    ctx.register_command("mystatus", handler=_handle_status, description="Show plugin status")

code_examples.caption: pre_llm_call context injection

gotchas: Handlers MUST return a JSON string — never a dict or other type
Handlers MUST accept **kwargs for forward compatibility — missing this breaks if Hermes passes extra context
Handlers should never raise exceptions — catch all and return error JSON
Plugin directory layout: ~/.hermes/plugins/<plugin-name>/plugin.yaml (max one level of category nesting)
Plugins are opt-in — run hermes plugins enable <name> before they appear in the banner
pre_llm_call context is injected into the user message (not system prompt) to preserve prompt cache
Built-in slash commands (help, model, new, etc.) cannot be overridden — registration is silently rejected
override=True is required to replace a built-in tool — explicit opt-in prevents accidental overwrites
full_text:
# Build a Hermes Plugin

This guide walks through building a complete Hermes plugin from scratch. By the end you'll have a working plugin with multiple tools, lifecycle hooks, shipped data files, and a bundled skill.
